facts_disclosed: Repaired ACL still stiff in cold weather

turns.content: man its absolutely chucking it down in bristol today. like proper grey miserable december weather you know. cant even be bothered to leave the house
December in Bristol does that. Classic grey skies and constant rain. Are you stuck inside for the day or do you have things you need to get out for?
i should be doing revision tbh but my knee is absolutely killing me today and its hard to focus when youre in pain init. days like this make it proper stiff and achey
That sounds rough. Is it an old injury or something more recent? The cold and damp weather can definitely aggravate joint issues.

nah its old. had my ACL repaired years ago and the surgery went well the knee is solid structurally but when its cold and rainy like today it just locks up you know. like i can feel every degree the temperature drops in that knee lol. proper weather forecast in my leg

Ha, the human barometer knee. That's actually really common with ACL repairs -- the scar tissue and the graft respond to changes in barometric pressure and cold. Have you found anything that helps on days like this?

yeah i mean i know all the textbook stuff init because im literally studying this haha. heat pack compression gentle movement. but theres something properly ironic about being a physio student with a dodgy knee. like i spend all day learning how to help other peoples bodies and then come home and my own knee is like nah bruv not today

The cobbler's shoes are always the worst, right? Though honestly, having first-hand experience with a serious knee injury probably makes you a better physio student. You actually know what it feels like to have a joint that doesn't cooperate.

ngl thats true. when a patient tells me their knee is stiff i dont have to imagine it i literally know. like i feel you mate mine does the same thing. 25 years old with an old mans knee though you know. my nan moves better than me some days and shes got nearly 55 years on me haha

That is quite the image. But seriously, that empathy is an asset. A lot of clinicians can explain the biomechanics but haven't lived through the frustration of a body that doesn't do what you want it to. Are you managing to keep up with any exercises for it or does a day like today just need to be a rest day?

tbh on days like this i just accept it. i do my normal stuff most of the time like quad sets and that but when the weathers like this its more about management than gains you know. heat pack on the knee cup of tea blanket and just ride it out. very glamorous life of a third year physio student lol

That sounds like a solid plan honestly. Sometimes the smart move is knowing when to back off. Heat pack, tea, and revision from the sofa -- not a bad December Saturday. What are you meant to be revising?

anatomy. always anatomy. theres always more anatomy to learn bruv its never ending. but yeah im going to put a heat pack on and try to get through some flashcards. if i can convince my knee to let me concentrate. cheers for the chat man even just moaning about it helps haha
Sometimes you just need to vent about it. Hope the knee loosens up as the day goes on -- stay warm and good luck with the anatomy revision. The heat pack is doing double duty: physio and comfort.
